docs.title: Claude API Documentation
docs.text:
Welcome to this community documentation project for the Anthropic Claude API.

This documentation was built as a learning project to practice API documentation and Docs-as-Code workflows using MkDocs and GitHub Pages.

docs.title: What is the Claude API?
docs.text: The Claude API allows developers to integrate Anthropic's Claude AI models into their own applications. You can send messages, receive responses, and build powerful AI-powered tools.

docs.title: What you will find here
docs.text:
• Authentication – How to set up your API key
  • Endpoints – Detailed reference for all available endpoints
  • Examples – Real code examples to get you started

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
1. Get your API key from the Anthropic Console
  2. Read the Authentication guide
  3. Make your first API call using the Examples section

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: The Claude API uses API keys to authenticate requests. You can manage your API keys in the Anthropic Console.

docs.title: Getting your API key
docs.text:
1. Go to console.anthropic.com
  2. Sign in or create an account
  3. Navigate to API Keys
  4. Click Create Key
  5. Copy your key and store it safely

!!! warning Never share your API key publicly or commit it to GitHub. Treat it like a password.

docs.title: Using your API key
docs.text:
Include your API key in every request as a header:

x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY
anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
Content-Type: application/json

docs.title: What is a Header?
docs.text: A header is extra information you send alongside your API request. Think of it like an envelope – the header is written on the outside and tells the server who you are before it opens the message.

docs.title: Messages
docs.text: The Messages endpoint is the core of the Claude API. It allows you to send messages to Claude and receive a response.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text: POST https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages

docs.title: Required headers
docs.text:
x-api-key: YOUR_API_KEY
anthropic-version: 2023-06-01
Content-Type: application/json

docs.title: Request body
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description model string ✅ The Claude model to use messages array ✅ List of messages in the conversation max_tokens integer ✅ Maximum number of tokens in the response

docs.title: Example request
docs.text:
{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "max_tokens": 1024,
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Hello, Claude!"
    }
  ]
}

docs.title: Example response
docs.text:
{
  "id": "msg_abc123",
  "type": "message",
  "role": "assistant",
  "content": [
    {
      "type": "text",
      "text": "Hello! How can I help you today?"
    }
  ],
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "stop_reason": "end_turn"
}

docs.title: What is a token?
docs.text: A token is a small piece of text – roughly 4 characters or three-quarters of a word. Claude counts both your input and its response in tokens. The max_tokens parameter controls how long Claude's response can be.

docs.title: Models
docs.text: Anthropic offers different Claude models optimized for different use cases. Each model has its own strengths, speed, and pricing.

docs.title: Available models
docs.text: Model Best for Context window claude-opus-4-6 Complex reasoning, long tasks 200K tokens claude-sonnet-4-6 Balanced speed and intelligence 200K tokens claude-haiku-4-5 Fast, lightweight tasks 200K tokens

docs.title: What is a context window?
docs.text:
The context window is the maximum amount of text Claude can read at once – including your messages and its own responses. Think of it like Claude's short-term memory.

200K tokens ≈ roughly 150,000 words ≈ an entire novel.

docs.title: Which model should I use?
docs.text:
• Just starting out? → Use claude-sonnet-4-6 Best balance of speed, quality, and cost.

  • Complex tasks or long documents? → Use claude-opus-4-6 Most powerful, but slower and more expensive.

  • Speed is critical? → Use claude-haiku-4-5 Fastest response time, ideal for simple tasks.

docs.title: How to specify a model
docs.text:
Include the model name in your request body:

{
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "max_tokens": 1024,
  "messages": [...]
}

docs.title: Basic Request
docs.text: This example shows how to make your first request to the Claude API using Python.

docs.title: What you need
docs.text:
• An Anthropic API key
  • Python installed on your computer
  • The Anthropic Python library

docs.title: Install the library
docs.text:
Open your terminal and run:

pip install anthropic

docs.title: The code
docs.text:
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")

message = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello, Claude!"}
    ]
)

print(message.content[0].text)

docs.title: What each line does
docs.text:
import anthropic Loads the Anthropic library so you can use it in your code.

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY") Creates a connection to the Claude API using your API key. Replace YOUR_API_KEY with your actual key from the Console.

client.messages.create(...) Sends the POST request to /v1/messages – this is where Claude receives your message and generates a response.

print(message.content[0].text) Prints Claude's response to your terminal.

docs.title: Expected output
docs.text: Hello! How can I help you today?

docs.title: Important
docs.text:
Never write your API key directly in your code like this in a real project. Use environment variables instead:

import os
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))

This keeps your key safe and out of your code.

docs.title: Streaming
docs.text: By default, Claude generates the complete response before sending it back. With streaming, Claude sends the response word by word as it generates it — just like you see in Claude.ai.

docs.title: Why use streaming?
docs.text:
• Users see the response immediately instead of waiting
  • Better experience for long responses
  • Makes your app feel faster and more responsive

docs.title: The code
docs.text:
import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")

with client.messages.stream(
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[
        {"role": "user", "content": "Tell me a short story."}
    ]
) as stream:
    for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end="", flush=True)

docs.title: What is different from a basic request?
docs.text: Basic Request Streaming Method messages.create() messages.stream() Response All at once Word by word Best for Short responses Long responses User experience Wait then read Read while generating

docs.title: What each line does
docs.text:
with client.messages.stream(...) as stream: Opens a streaming connection to the Claude API. The with block ensures the connection closes cleanly when done.

for text in stream.text_stream: Loops through each piece of text as Claude generates it.

print(text, end="", flush=True) Prints each piece immediately without a line break. flush=True makes sure it appears instantly in the terminal.
